# hrobot_server (Data Source)

Reads facts about a Hetzner Robot dedicated server. Look the server up by `number` (the Robot server-number) or by `ip` (any IPv4 address bound to the server). Exactly one of the two must be set.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "hrobot_server" "by_number" {
  number = 2893
}

data "hrobot_server" "by_ip" {
  ip = "176.9.18.203"
}

output "server_product" {
  value = data.hrobot_server.by_number.product
}

output "server_number_from_ip" {
  value = data.hrobot_server.by_ip.number
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `ip` (String) An IPv4 address bound to the server, used to resolve its server-number. Mutually exclusive with `number`.
- `number` (Number) The Robot server-number. Set this to look the server up directly; left unset, it is resolved from `ip`.

### Read-Only

- `cancelled` (Boolean) Whether the server is cancelled.
- `datacenter` (String) The datacenter the server is located in (e.g. FSN1-DC8).
- `id` (String) The Robot server-number as a string.
- `ips` (List of String) All single IP addresses bound to the server.
- `ipv4` (String) The primary IPv4 address of the server.
- `ipv6_network` (String) The IPv6 network assigned to the server.
- `name` (String) The server name set in Robot.
- `paid_until` (String) The date the server is paid until (YYYY-MM-DD).
- `product` (String) The product name (e.g. AX41, EQ 8).
- `status` (String) The server status (e.g. ready, in process).
- `traffic` (String) The traffic limit for the server.
